data.narrative:
Mach Industries is a Series B-stage frontier-tech company in Defense & Security that has raised $185M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—all at Series B stage within the same sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $185M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a valuation of $352M. The peer-median method examines 7 same-stage, same-sector private companies including Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), and Castelion and Defense Unicorns (both $1.0B). The median across this set is $1.0B, with a range spanning $300M to $3.0B. These two methods triangulate to a low of $299M, a median of $676M, and a high of $1.1B. The substantial spread reflects meaningful dispersion within the peer set—a 10x range from floor to ceiling. Key uncertainties include the degree to which Mach's technology maturity, customer traction, and team pedigree align with higher-quartile peers versus lower-quartile peers, and whether Defense & Security frontier-tech multiples will sustain or compress in the coming 12–18 months.
